name: MEDITECH FHIR API
what_it_does: FHIR R4 REST API providing access to clinical data in MEDITECH hospital information systems, covering 2,300+ acute care hospitals and health systems worldwide.
use_cases: Patient access apps for hospital patients (21st Century Cures compliance)
Care transition and discharge summary integrations
Clinical analytics over inpatient and acute care data
Provider-facing clinical workflow tools integrated with hospital EHR
Population health management over hospital encounter data
not_for: Ambulatory/outpatient-only workflows (MEDITECH focuses on acute care)
Direct administrative or billing system access
Organizations without MEDITECH-deployed health systems as partners
best_when: Building apps that need access to inpatient/acute care data from community hospitals and regional health systems on MEDITECH, particularly for patient access mandated by ONC rules.
avoid_when: You need ambulatory EHR data, your targets are large academic medical centers (more likely Epic/Cerner), or you need a rich developer ecosystem with community support.

auth.notes: SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0 for both patient-facing and provider-facing access. Each hospital deploys its own authorization server. Requires registration with individual health systems. No centralized developer program.

pricing.paid_tiers: Access negotiated directly with each MEDITECH-using health system; MEDITECH itself does not offer a direct developer program
pricing.notes: No central MEDITECH developer portal. Access is through individual health system agreements. Some health systems may have sandbox environments.

performance.rate_limits: Per health system, not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: FHIR OperationOutcome resources returned; detail quality varies by health system deployment
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET operations idempotent; write support limited in FHIR API
known_agent_gotchas: No centralized developer portal — must negotiate separately with each hospital
SMART on FHIR browser-redirect OAuth is incompatible with autonomous agent flows
FHIR API coverage is narrower than Epic or Cerner; fewer resource types supported
Documentation is sparse compared to competitor EHR vendors
HIPAA BAA required per health system before any PHI access
Rate limits and capabilities vary significantly across MEDITECH deployments

security_score_components.security_notes: HIPAA-regulated health systems, SMART on FHIR OAuth, TLS enforced; security posture varies by individual hospital deployment
